business.headline: Are you using your no claim bonus smartly or losing money?By treating a no-claim bonus as a strategic asset rather than a simple discount, you can protect your savings and ensure that your insurance remains both affordable and comprehensive.

Sensex sinks 852 points, Nifty slips below 24,200; Dr Reddy's jumps nearly 9%The BSE Sensex closed at 77,664.00, down 852.49 points or 1.09 per cent. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,173.05, lower by 205.05 points or 0.84 per cent, slipping below the 24,200 mark.

Bikaji founder Shiv Ratan Agarwal passes away at 75Shiv Ratan Agarwal, founder of Bikaji Foods, died at 75 in Chennai, leaving behind a legacy of transforming a regional snack business into a globally recognised brand.

Thought UAE free zones meant 0% corporate tax? Here's the realityThe idea of zero corporate tax has made the UAE a popular choice for many investors. But the promise of 0% tax is not as straightforward as it seems, and comes with conditions many often overlook.

Tax clearance for foreign travel: Do you need it every time you leave India?Section 420 of the Income-tax Act, 2025 deals with tax clearance requirements for certain individuals leaving India. While the provision exists in the new law, it is not intended to apply universally.

Just 1-2% traders drive 70% India's F&O volumes: Nithin Kamath warns of imbalanceIndia's booming derivatives market may look large from the outside, but the reality is far more concentrated. Zerodha's Nithin Kamath says a tiny group of traders is driving most of the activity, raising concerns about balance.

Crude oil above $100 again: Will petrol, diesel get costlier soon?Global crude oil prices climbed above $100 per barrel on Thursday, raising a question millions of Indian consumers care about most. Will this lead to costlier petrol and diesel prices?

Rupee slides to 94 against US dollar: Is crude oil the biggest risk right now?The rupee has already fallen nearly 1% this week and has slipped well past its recent highs of around 92.50. That earlier rise was largely supported by measures taken by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in late March and April.

Dr Reddy's shares jump 10% as Sensex falls: Why is the pharma stock rising?Shares of Dr Reddy's Laboratories surged sharply on Thursday, extending gains to 10% even as Dalal Street remained under pressure amid rising crude oil prices and global uncertainty.

Sensex tumbles 800 points: Why is the stock market falling?Dalal Street fell sharply on Thursday as crude oil surged above $100, banking and auto stocks declined, and investors worried about the economic fallout from the West Asia conflict.
Sensex, Nifty sink as crude oil prices reach $100; IndiGo down 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was down by 625.09 points to 77,891.40, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 173.90 points to 24,204.20 as of 9:26 am.

Stock market today: Will rising crude and Iran tensions drag Sensex, Nifty down?Early indicators suggest a weak start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,138 as of 8:30 am, pointing to a gap-down opening for the Nifty 50, which had closed at 24,378.1 in the previous session.

Brent crude crosses $100 as Iran tensions rise, peace talks stallOil prices have now risen for a fourth straight session, supported by larger-than-expected draws in US gasoline and distillate inventories, indicating robust demand.

Gold at Rs 1.52 lakh, silver near Rs 2.47 lakh: Should you stay invested or sell?Gold and silver prices are moving sharply, and many investors are wondering what to do next. With gold around Rs 1.52 lakh and silver near Rs 2.47 lakh, is it time to stay invested or consider selling?
Govt allows ethanol blending in Aviation Turbine FuelThe move is aimed at promoting Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and reducing India's dependence on imported oil.
Why more roads won't fix Gurugram's traffic crisisGurugram's population has doubled from 1.5 million in 2011 to over 3 million today. Only 12 to 15% of residents regularly use public transport.
Sensex ends 757 points lower, Nifty below 24,400; HCLTech sinks 11%The S&P BSE Sensex tanked 756.84 points to close at 78,516.49, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 198.50 points to end at 24,378.10.

Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights to save fuel costs as prices surgeJet fuel is one of the largest cost components for airlines, and a surge in prices has made several short-haul routes unviable. To manage this, Lufthansa is pulling back from unprofitable routes.

Do you need to file ITR if you over 80? Here's what you should knowTurning 80 comes with a few tax relaxations, but does it mean you can skip filing your ITR altogether? The rules are not that simple. Here's what you should know before assuming you are exempt.

Petrol, diesel prices unchanged in India. Will they rise soon?The West Asia war has triggered a major global crude oil supply shock, but India's petrol and diesel rates have remained unchanged since May 2022. We spoke to experts to understand how much longer India can keep fuel prices steady.

entertainment.headline: Raja Shivaji trailer revives interest in Shivaji's Wagh Nakh. All about the weapon The Raja Shivaji trailer brings the Wagh Nakh into focus once more. Riteish Deshmukh as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj wields the iconic weapon, long seen as a symbol of strategy and power.

Governor first poster out: Manoj Bajpayee fronts a tense nation-in-crisis tale Manoj Bajpayee's new film Governor: The Silent Saviour, was announced on his 57th birthday. The film is directed by Chinmay Mandlekar.

Prime Video, ZEE5, JioHotstar: 24, Marty Supreme, Pratichaya and more Prime Video, ZEE5 and JioHotstar have an exciting lineup of releases this week across genres and languages. From Marty Supreme and Pratichaya to 24, here's what you can stream.

All about Ramayana's grand opening scene: Ravana-Kubera's epic battle Ramayana is set to begin on an unexpected note, shifting focus away from the familiar Rama–Ravana face-off. Instead, the film's opening draws from a lesser-known episode that could redefine how audiences see Ravana's rise and ambition.

Netflix this week: Nukkad Naatak, Apex, If Wishes Could Kill and more Netflix is bringing a packed slate of releases this week across genres and languages. From Nukkad Naatak and Apex to If Wishes Could Kill, here's everything premiering on the platform.

Avinash Tiwary on Dhurandhar's box office success, depiction of alpha men in cinemas Avinash Tiwary discussed the success of Dhurandhar and the importance of family audiences returning to theatres for Bollywood's revival. He also reflected on the changing portrayal of masculinity in Hindi cinema, emphasising integrity over traditional labels like 'alpha' or 'soft' men.

Actor Divyanka Sirohi's 'Shiva take me with you' post goes viral after her death Haryanvi actor Divyanka Sirohi's sudden death at 30 has left fans in shock, with an old Instagram post now resurfacing and going viral. As tributes pour in, the cryptic "Shiva take me with you" caption is drawing renewed attention online.

SS Rajamouli's Varanasi set for global debut at Mexico ComicCon 2026 SS Rajamouli's Varanasi will be showcased at CCXP Mexico (Comic Con Experience) with exclusive content and a team interaction. The film features Mahesh Babu, Priyanka Chopra and Prithviraj Sukumaran in key roles.

Michael review: Jaafar Jackson embodies MJ in a soulful but sanitised biopicRelease Date:Fri Apr 24, 2026 Michael, starring Jaafar Jackson, is a dazzling spectacle that celebrates the legend while sidestepping the man – hitting the high notes but muting the icon's most difficult truths.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 6: Akshay brings 3rd biggest Bollywood film of 2026 Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla is off to a solid start at the box office, climbing the ranks among the year's top-grossing films within days of release. As the horror-comedy builds momentum globally, can it sustain its run and challenge bigger hits like Border 2?

Inside Sehar Hone Ko Hai row: Trolls, tension, tiff surrounding actors Parth-Rishita The off-screen tensions between actors Parth Samthaan and Rishita Kothari from Sehar Hone Ko Hai have stirred a storm of trolling and speculation, impacting their professional camaraderie.

Row over Lawrence Bishnoi docuseries: Congress MP seeks ban, cites Moosewala's killing Congress MP Amarinder Singh Raja Warring has demanded a ban on the upcoming docuseries Lawrence of Punjab, alleging it glorifies gangster Lawrence Bishnoi and disrespects victims like Sidhu Moosewala.

Samay Raina's Still Alive becomes the most-watched comedy special in the world Samay Raina's comeback special Still Alive has become the most-watched full-length stand-up comedy special on YouTube. The Special had 53.5 million views on April 22.

Clayface teaser trailer: DC's eerie new villain takes shape in dark body horror DC Studios has unveiled the trailer for Clayface, a gripping psychological body horror film that explores the origin story of Batman's lesser-known villain. Featuring Tom Rhys Harries and Naomi Ackie, the movie promises a chilling narrative set for release in October.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 35: Rs 46 crore short of Baahubali 2's 9-year-old record Dhurandhar 2 continues its historic box office run, eyeing Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's long-standing global record. With just Rs 46 crore left to bridge the gap, can Dhurandhar: The Revenge pull off one of Indian cinema's biggest coups?

Russell Brand reacts to Katy Perry dating Justin Trudeau: KP, you took it too far Russell Brand joked about ex-wife Katy Perry's reported relationship with Justin Trudeau, saying she "took it too far." He also reflected on their failed marriage, admitting that his insecurities and obsession with fame contributed to their divorce.

Rajinikanth's early morning stroll trends as he casts his vote on polling day Superstar Rajinikanth's morning walk in Chennai went viral on polling day, drawing attention online. He later cast his vote at Stella Maris College in Chennai, along with his daughter Soundarya Rajinikanth.

Ajith braves chaotic crowd to caste vote in Tamil Nadu elections. Viral videos Tamil star and racer Ajith Kumar was among the first voters in Chennai's Thiruvanmiyur booth during the Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections. He returned recently from Belgium after finishing second in a 24-hour endurance race.

Won at Cannes, now leads jury: India's Payal Kapadia takes over Critics' Week A year after All We Imagine As Light made history at Cannes, Payal Kapadia has landed another major honour at the festival. She will now head the jury at the 65th Cannes Critics' Week, which spotlights emerging talent from across the world.

general news.headline: Video: How masked Iranian commandos seized India-bound ship in HormuzA day after two merchant vessels were seized, including one bound for Gujarat, Iran released dramatic footage showing masked commandos taking control of the ships.

BJP chief won't be able to save you: Mahua Moitra outs location of leadersIn a sharply worded post on X, Mahua Moitra shared an image featuring BJP leaders Saurabh Singh and Swadesh Singh. Both individuals were circled in red. Moitra also revealed their locations and issued a stark warning.

Ex-help dragged IRS officer's daughter down the stairs for fingerprint to open safeDuring interrogation, the accused revealed that he dragged the unconscious victim from a rooftop study room down the stairs to a lower floor in an attempt to use her fingerprints to access a safe.

Only Muslim, only Hindu tenants: Sting Op exposes illegal ads by Indians in LondonAn investigative report by a UK-based daily has exposed illegal social media advertisements for rental properties, seeking tenants on the basis of religion. The ads, which sought 'only Hindu' and 'only Muslim' tenants, are illegal under the Equality Act 2010 in the UK.

Planned for 10 years, fled in 2 hours: How family of 9 escaped North Korea by seaA family's audacious plan for freedom from a dictatorial North Korea took a decade of preparation before they crossed the Yellow Sea into South Korea's waters. From learning to fish to hiding in plain sight, nine members of the Kim family escaped from North Korea on a stormy night. Here's how.

Live: PM accuses TMC of blocking Centre's work, says funds not reaching peopleAssembly Election Live Updates: West Bengal's first-phase elections witnessed multiple incidents of tension, allegations and disruptions across districts. In Murshidabad, clashes broke out between TMC workers and AUJP founder Humayun Kabir near a site earlier hit by crude bomb violence, while residents expressed fear after an injured woman triggered security concerns. Separate clashes between TMC and BJP workers were reported in Siliguri, prompting CAPF intervention. In Kumarganj, BJP candidate Subhendu Sarkar alleged removal of polling agents from several booths and claimed he was attacked during inspection, blaming TMC workers. In Keshpur, a 55-year-old woman died after voting, with family citing missing voter list entries and shock after leaving the booth. Meanwhile, voting went on smoothly and peacefully in Tamil Nadu as several celebrities and top political leaders cast their ballot. Tamil Nadu is voting in a single phase to elect representatives for all its 234 seats. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Sporadic clashes mar Bengal Phase 1 voting; 70% turnout in Tamil Nadu till 3 pm Live UpdatesPolling is underway across 152 of West Bengal's 294 constituencies in the first phase, while all 234 seats in Tamil Nadu are voting in a single-day contest. Both states are expected to see pitched poll battles that could reshape regional power equations.

I ate jhalmuri, Trinamool felt the heat: PM jabs Mamata Banerjee as Bengal votesJhalmuri has emerged as a political flashpoint in Bengal after PM Modi's roadside stop in Jhargram sparked a slugfest with TMC. At a Krishnanagar rally, he took a swipe at Mamata Banerjee's party, saying while he ate jhalmuri, it was TMC that felt the jolt.

I am a doctor for diabetes, BP, cholesterol and this is my must-do morning routineA healthy lifestyle adopted today is like a long-term investment, writes Dr Chandrakant Lahariya in this edition of DocYard. He adds, it pays dividends in the form of vitality, independence, and quality of life as you age.

Trump's 'hellhole' mention for India, China as he targets birthright citizenshipDonald Trump posted a controversial letter on Truth Social calling India, China and other nations "hellholes" while attacking birthright citizenship and alleging immigrants exploit US systems.

Humayun Kabir vs TMC: Smashed windscreen, stones hurled, voter intimidation claimsClashes broke out in Murshidabad during early polling as Bharatpur MLA Humayun Kabir was attacked near a booth in Naoda. Kabir, now with his own party after suspension from TMC, accused ruling party workers of intimidation, prompting the Election Commission to seek a report on the incident.

Too machh concern or mockery? What Bengalis think of BJP's fish campaignBJP leaders have campaigned on Bengal's streets holding fish, while leaders like Anurag Thakur were seen enjoying machh bhaat during visits. Himanta Biswa Sarma went a step ahead and challenged Mamata Banerjee to a fish-eating contest, and even PM Modi mentioned fish in his speech. The tactics vary, but the message is the same: we are one of you. The question is: does Bengal buy it?
Iran's social media WMDs- Lego shortsHow Iran reverse engineered Hollywood adaptations of a European toymaker to perfect its own Weapon of Mass Distraction.

Asim Munir's double game? Behind Pakistan's flop show in US-Iran talksWith a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran in limbo, Pakistan's credibility as a mediator has come under scrutiny. The failure of Pakistan, especially its army chief, Asim Munir, to secure guarantees from an erratic Trump has led to a growing mistrust with Iran.

Pir Panjal is fenced. How do Pak terrorists still cross into J&K?The mountains and forests of Pir Panjal do not obey man-made lines, but the Indian Army heavily guards this border along the LoC. It is now fenced and watched round the clock. But how do Pakistani terrorists still manage to cross into Jammu and Kashmir, and massacre civilians like they did in Pahalgam?

Bengal BJP leader claims ink over lotus symbol on EVM, CRPF says issue fixedThe BJP leader flagged an issue of ink being smeared over the party's Lotus symbol on an EVM at a polling booth in West Bengal's Raghunathpur. The CRPF promptly intervened, cleaned the ink and confirmed that voting continued peacefully thereafter.

Rapido driver receives dog to deliver as 'parcel', watch for instant serotonin boostA Rapido driver found himself delivering the "cutest parcel ever" which turned out to be an adorable dog, leaving the internet completely smitten.

Live: Pakistan, US discuss Iran talks in Islamabad amid ceasefire pushUS-Iran War Live Updates: Senior Iranian officials have accused Washington of stalling peace negotiations, citing a US naval blockade of Iranian ports as a key obstacle, while Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it seized two foreign vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and fired on a third for alleged maritime violations. President Masoud Pezeshkian said Tehran remains open to "dialogue and agreement" but that breaches of commitments, blockades and threats are hindering meaningful talks. US President Donald Trump has signalled he remains prepared to resume military action if negotiations fail. Meanwhile, Lebanon condemned Israel over the killing of journalist Amal Khalil and the wounding of her colleague Zeinab Faraj, accusing it of violating a ceasefire through continued strikes and demolitions. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

US Navy Secretary fired weeks after Army chief in sweeping shake-up amid Iran warUS Navy Secretary John Phelan has been fired amid tensions with Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth, slow shipbuilding reforms, and an ethics probe, during US-Iran war.

Dr Reddy's shares jump 10% as Sensex falls: Why is the pharma stock rising?Shares of Dr Reddy's Laboratories surged sharply on Thursday, extending gains to 10% even as Dalal Street remained under pressure amid rising crude oil prices and global uncertainty.

PM Modi's shoutout to Indian cheesemakers on their big win in BrazilIndian cheesemakers won four medals at the Mundial do Queijo do Brasil 2026, an international cheese competition held in São Paulo, Brazil.

Videographer filming bride's farewell flung into air by car, killed on spotA road accident in Sirsa was caught on camera during a wedding farewell, where a 25-year-old photographer was fatally hit by a speeding car while filming the bride's farewell on Hisar Road.

Triangular turn in Tamil Nadu: Vijay disrupts decades-old DMK vs AIADMK contestAs Tamil Nadu votes today, the polls mark a historic shift from a long-standing DMK–AIADMK bipolar contest to an unpredictable triangular battle, with Vijay's TVK rewriting the electoral equations.

Butter chicken tsunami: New Zealand MP's racist rant over India trade dealShane Jones's "butter chicken tsunami" remarks drew strong criticism from the Indian community and political leaders, and also exposed divisions within the coalition over immigration concerns and the parliamentary backing needed to pass the free trade deal with India.

sports.headline: RCB's Nuwan Thushara withdraws case against Sri Lanka Cricket over IPL 2026 NOC Fast bowler Nuwan Thushara, who had dragged Sri Lanka Cricket to court for not issuing him a No Objection Certificate (NoC) to compete in the Indian Premier League, on Thursday withdrew the case against the governing body.

I'm sorry, boss: Ex-India selector apologises to Rohit for 2011 World Cup snub Kris Srikkanth said he apologised to Rohit Sharma for leaving him out of India's 2011 ICC Cricket World Cup squad. He explained that the selectors chose an all-rounder-heavy team inspired by the 1983 Cricket World Cup model.

Stay in India Bethell, Cook knows nothing of IPL: Pietersen fires at ex-teammate IPL 2026: Kevin Pietersen has defended Jacob Bethell's IPL stint, taking aim at Alastair Cook's criticism. The former England captains remain divided on whether learning in the IPL outweighs the need for regular match time.

Premier League weekend preview: Arsenal, Spurs and Man United eye crucial wins Arsenal, Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester United head into crucial Premier League clashes with pressure mounting, as injuries, form and league positions shape key encounters.

Bottle-job in sight for Arsenal? Manchester City back on top after Burnley win Manchester City seized the Premier League lead from Arsenal following a narrow 1-0 victory over Burnley, marking the first time they have topped the table since the opening week.

Mayank Yadav criticism horrifying: Bishop defends LSG pacer's costly IPL return IPL 2026: Ian Bishop has backed Mayank Yadav after criticism over his IPL return. The former pacer called the backlash "horrifying" and urged fans to focus on the youngster's recovery and long-term fitness.

Mitchell Starc cleared to play IPL: DC pacer expected to be available for RR clash IPL 2026: Mitchell Starc has been cleared to return to IPL 2026 after recovering from injury. The Delhi Capitals pacer is set to rejoin the squad and could feature in their upcoming clash against Rajasthan Royals.

India eye strong Thomas & Uber Cup run as badminton heads to Denmark India head into the Thomas & Uber Cup 2026 with a mix of proven stars and rising talent. With Ayush Shetty's recent surge and Sindhu leading the women, the focus will be on how far these squads can go.

IPL's war against left-arm spin and what Ravindra Jadeja makes of it IPL 2026: The IPL's reliance on match-ups has reshaped how left-arm spinners are used, often at the cost of context. Ravindra Jadeja weighs in on the balance between data, conditions, and on-field judgement.

Cartoons first, bowlers next: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reveals his IPL routine at 15 IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to grab attention with his performances and personality in IPL 2026. The 15-year-old's revelation about watching cartoons before matches adds a unique layer to his growing story.

Rajasthan Royals' Nandre Burger fined for abusive send off to LSG's Rishabh Pant IPL 2026: Nandre Burger, pacer for Rajasthan Royals, was fined for breaching the Indian Premier League Code of Conduct after an abusive send-off to Rishabh Pant. He was also handed one demerit point for his behaviour.

Donald Trump envoy pushes FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup 2026FIFA World Cup 2026 faces fresh controversy after a proposal to replace Iran with Italy from none other than USA President Donald Trump's envoy. While the suggestion has sparked debate, FIFA has not indicated any change to the current tournament lineup.

Lamine Yamal's World Cup 2026 hopes in doubt after FC Barcelona injury scareLamine Yamal suffered an injury scare during Barcelona's La Liga clash, raising concerns ahead of a crucial phase. The young star now faces a race against time to recover before the FIFA World Cup 2026.

MI vs CSK: Will MS Dhoni spice up strange IPL El Clasico of fallen giants?IPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings face off in IPL 2026's first El Clasico on Thursday, but this time the contest carries a different edge, with both five-time champions still searching for consistency. The spotlight, as ever, is on MS Dhoni, who is on the verge of a return after a calf strain, while Rohit Sharma is also pushing to come back from a hamstring injury.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: How Rishabh Pant's stupid swipe sank Lucknow at homeIPL 2026, LSG vs RR: Rishabh Pant lasted just three balls, but that brief stay set the tone for a muddled batting effort from Lucknow Super Giants. Chasing 160, they squandered the hard work of their fast bowlers and never truly looked in control against Rajasthan Royals.

Laura Wolvaardt's South Africa outclass India to take 3-0 series leadSouth Africa secured an unassailable 3-0 lead in the five-match T20I series with a commanding nine-wicket win over India in the third match at the Wanderers Stadium in Johannesburg on Wednesday.

BCCI is killing Indian players: Lalit Modi lashes out at boardFormer IPL boss, Lalit Modi has lashed out at the Indian Cricket Board, calling it heartless. Modi has said that BCCI treats its players like mules and is killing them with hectic scheduling.

Thanks for being soft: Pant cuts a sorry figure after inexplicable loss vs RRRishabh Pant's Lucknow Super Giants had a game to forget against Rajasthan Royals on April 23. Playing at home, Pant's side failed to chase down 160 runs, handing a 40-run win to visitors Rajasthan.

Jadeja's POTM gesture explained: Why did he thank Gujarat's Education Minister?Ravindra Jadeja turned in a match-winning all-round performance for Rajasthan Royals in their IPL 2026 clash against Lucknow Super Giants at the Ekana Stadium on Wednesday, but it was his post-match gesture that truly grabbed attention.

IPL 2026: No one-man show as Jadeja and Archer leave LSG lost at homeIPL 2026, LSG vs RR: Lucknow Super Giants lost their seventh successive game at home, failing to chase a 160-run target at the Ekana Stadium on Wednesday. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi did not fire, but Rajasthan clicked as a unit to snap their two-match losing streak.

Chelsea sack manager Liam Rosenior after five straight Premier League defeatsChelsea FC have dismissed head coach Liam Rosenior after a tenure of less than four months, following a run of five consecutive defeats in the Premier League without scoring, a sequence not seen at the club since 1912.

Every ball Mayank Yadav bowled on his IPL returnMayank Yadav made his return to the Indian Premier League after a hiatus of 353 days. Back from a serious injury break, Mayank bowled his full quota of 4 overs on Wednesday. Here's how it went for the tearaway fast bowler.

Tilak Varma explains how Rohit Sharma's tip sparked his masterclass against GTIPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Tilak Varma revealed on Wednesday that Rohit Sharma's advice to play the first 15–20 balls without overthinking the situation helped him return to form with an IPL hundred.

LSG vs RR: Hundreds traveled for a glimpse, but no Sooryavanshi party in LucknowIPL 2026, LSG vs RR: The buzz around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was unmistakable in Lucknow on Wednesday. Several fans travelled from afar to watch the 15-year-old in action, but he endured a rare off day, scoring just 8 against Lucknow Super Giants.

Puducherry cricket body suspends 3 players on charges of assault on U-19 state coachThe Cricket Association of Pondicherry (CAP) has suspended three of its first-class cricketers with immediate effect on allegations that they assaulted U-19 state team coach Venkataraman on December 8 last year.

LSG vs RR: After 353-day injury layoff, pacer Mayank Yadav makes IPL returnLucknow Super Giants received a timely boost as tearaway pacer Mayank Yadav returned to the playing XI after a 353-day injury layoff, with captain Rishabh Pant opting to bowl first against Rajasthan Royals at the Ekana Stadium on Tuesday.

MI vs CSK: MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma face late fitness tests ahead of El ClasicoIPL 2026, MI vs CSK: MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma are set to undergo late fitness tests after the two stalwarts batted in tandem during a nets session on Wednesday, ahead of the much-anticipated Mumbai Indians vs Chennai Super Kings clash.

LSG vs RR Highlights: Jofra Archer leads Rajasthan to epic comeback win at EkanaLucknow (LSG) vs Rajasthan (RR) Highlights, IPL 2026: LSG (119-all-out in 18 ovs), chasing 160. Rajasthan Royals stage an epic comeback at the Ekana Stadium in Lucknow. Defending just 159, with no luxury of an Impact Sub, the Rajasthan bowling attack bowled out the hosts for just 119 runs.

Real Madrid star Bellingham invests in cricket: Buys stake in Hundred franchiseJude Bellingham steps into cricket with a stake in Birmingham Phoenix. The Real Madrid star's investment reflects both his sporting interests and deep-rooted connection to Birmingham.

PAK star under investigation after testing positive for recreational drug useT20 World Cup 2026: Pakistan's Mohammad Nawaz faces a PCB probe after a reported positive drug test during the T20 World Cup, with his Surrey deal cancelled and his immediate cricketing future now uncertain.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
